user_query:
I’m preparing our Weekly Symbols Research Sync for 2026-04-22, focused on verifying official sources for symbols from post-Soviet states. Please use the provided backlog and template to plan the meeting, perform targeted web searches, and produce the agenda, findings, and a draft of meeting notes with action items.

Inputs (read-only):
- input/research_backlog.csv — list of research items to verify
- input/agenda_template.md — agenda template with placeholders
- input/attendees.csv — attendee names, roles, and expertise

What to do:
1) For each row in input/research_backlog.csv, perform at least one web search engine query that targets primary/official sources (e.g., national legal acts portals, presidential decrees, parliaments, official gazettes, heraldic councils/commissions, or national standards bodies). Open at least one relevant result per item when possible. Prefer official domains and primary documents over third-party summaries. Do not use or include direct URLs here; record only the source domain and page title.

2) Produce a structured findings table and a search log:
- Save output/findings/findings.csv with the exact columns:
  backlog_id,country,symbol_type,query_used,source_domain,document_title,accessed_date,status,notes
  Requirements:
  - status must be either "confirmed" (if you identified a plausible official primary source) or "pending" (if not found/uncertain), with a concise reason in notes.
  - accessed_date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD).
  - query_used should be the most effective query string you executed for that item.
- Save output/search_logs/search_log.json capturing, for each backlog_id:
  { "backlog_id": str, "queries": [str, ...], "sources": [{"domain": str, "title": str, "accessed_date": str}], "status_summary": str }
  Requirements:
  - Include every query you actually ran for the item (at least one per backlog row).
  - sources should list the sources you opened (if any). If none were suitable, keep it an empty array and explain in status_summary.

3) Edit the agenda from the template and prepare meeting notes:
- Fill input/agenda_template.md placeholders and save as output/agendas/agenda_2026-04-22.md
  - {{MEETING_DATE}}: 2026-04-22
  - {{ATTENDEES_LIST}}: comma-separated names from input/attendees.csv
  - {{OBJECTIVES}}: a short paragraph summarizing that we will: (a) review confirmed official sources, (b) triage pending items, and (c) assign follow-ups.
  - {{ITEMS_CONFIRMED}}: bullet list derived from output/findings/findings.csv where status == "confirmed"; each bullet must include [backlog_id] country — topic — source_domain.
  - {{ITEMS_PENDING}}: bullet list where status == "pending"; each bullet must include [backlog_id] country — topic — brief reason from notes.
  - {{ACTION_ITEMS}}: create an initial preview list proposing one concrete next step for each pending item (reference the backlog_id and suggest a likely official source to try next based on source_hint). Do not assign owners here.
- Create output/meeting/meeting_notes_2026-04-22.md with three sections:
  - Decisions: leave as TBD placeholders that can be filled live in the meeting.
  - Findings summary: two bullet lists that mirror the confirmed and pending items (use the same [backlog_id] references and short descriptions).
  - Action items: for each pending backlog_id, create an actionable task line with fields: backlog_id, task summary, owner, due (use 2026-04-29), and rationale. Assign owners by matching the item’s region/subject to the closest expertise in input/attendees.csv. If there is no clear match, assign to "A. Bot" as Research Coordinator.

Consistency and acceptance:
- Every backlog_id in input/research_backlog.csv must appear in both output/findings/findings.csv and output/search_logs/search_log.json.
- The agenda’s Confirmed/Pending sections must reflect the statuses in output/findings/findings.csv.
- Each action item in output/meeting/meeting_notes_2026-04-22.md must reference a backlog_id from the backlog and an owner name present in input/attendees.csv (or "A. Bot").

Deliverables to create:
- output/findings/findings.csv
- output/search_logs/search_log.json
- output/agendas/agenda_2026-04-22.md (edited from the provided template)
- output/meeting/meeting_notes_2026-04-22.md

Let me know if any item is clearly out of scope for official confirmation so we can re-prioritize in the meeting. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
